java::util::Enumeration Interface Reference

Inherited by java::util::Hashtable::Enumerator.

Public Member Functions

Object nextElement () throws NoSuchElementException

Package Functions

boolean hasMoreElements ()

Detailed Description

Interface for lists of objects that can be returned in sequence. Successive objects are obtained by the nextElement method.

As of Java 1.2, the Iterator interface provides the same functionality, but with shorter method names and a new optional method to remove items from the list. If writing for 1.2, consider using Iterator instead. Enumerations over the new collections classes, for use with legacy APIs that require them, can be obtained by the enumeration method in class Collections.

Author:
Warren Levy (warrenl@cygnus.com)
Eric Blake (ebb9@email.byu.edu)
See also:

Hashtable
Vector

Member Function Documentation

boolean java::util::Enumeration::hasMoreElements (  )  [package]

Tests whether there are elements remaining in the enumeration.

Returns:
true if there is at least one more element in the enumeration, that is, if the next call to nextElement will not throw a NoSuchElementException.
Object java::util::Enumeration::nextElement (  )  throws NoSuchElementException

Obtain the next element in the enumeration.

Returns:
the next element in the enumeration
Exceptions:
NoSuchElementException if there are no more elements
